name: Braised Duck with Orange-And-Lime Sauce (Zanzibar)
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Chicken stock, fresh or canned
1/2 cup Finely chopped sweet bell pepper, preferably red
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare orange wedges or slices studded with whole cloves for garnish.
- About 1 1/2 to 2 inches long, stemmed and seeded
- Be cautious when handling hot chili.
2. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
3. Pat the duck completely dry inside and out with paper towels.
4. Remove large chunks of fat from the cavity.
5. Cut off the loose neck skin and truss the bird securely.
6. Prick the surface around the thighs, back, and lower part of the breast with a skewer or sharp knife.
7. In a heavy 5- to 6-quart casserole, heat oil over moderate heat until a light haze forms above it.
8. Add the duck and turn frequently with a slotted spoon or tongs, cooking for about 15 minutes or until browned on all sides.
9. Transfer the duck to a plate and discard remaining fat in the casserole.
10. Pour in 1 cup of chicken stock and bring to a boil over high heat, scraping any brown particles from the bottom and sides of the pan.
11. Stir in cloves and chill.
12. Return the duck and accumulated liquids to the casserole.
13. Cover tightly and braise in the middle of the oven for 1 hour.
14. Remove the duck to a plate and skim as much fat as possible from the surface of the cooking liquid.
15. Discard cloves and chili.
16. Add remaining cup of stock to the casserole, stirring and scraping brown bits into the pan, then bring to a boil over high heat.
17. Mix in orange juice, lime juice, sweet bell pepper, and salt.
18. Return duck to the casserole and baste with simmering sauce.
19. Cover tightly and return duck to the oven for about 15 minutes.
20. Test for doneness by piercing thigh with a small, sharp knife; juices should be clear yellow if cooked properly.
21. Garnish platter with orange wedges or slices and serve immediately.
